title: Public Release Capabilities
description: The public release feature set focuses on login, trial or payment, Remnawave provisioning, config delivery, support recovery, and monitored operations.

quantum.title: Protocol Baseline
quantum.subtitle: VLESS Reality RAW + XHTTP
quantum.description: CyberVPN keeps the public VPN transport surface narrow: VLESS Reality RAW is the default profile and VLESS Reality XHTTP is the alternate path where configured and evidenced.
specs: Out of public default: Helix, Verta, Beep production

multihop.title: Evidence-Gated Regions
multihop.subtitle: Country-level public inventory
multihop.description: Regions are not public promises until staging or production node evidence exists. The catalogue hides unavailable nodes.

obfuscation.title: DPI-Resistant Profiles
obfuscation.subtitle: No universal unblock promise
obfuscation.description: Reality-based profiles are designed for restricted networks, but public copy stays bounded: performance and access depend on the user's network and current node evidence.

killswitch.title: Support Recovery Path
killswitch.subtitle: Payment and provisioning safeguards
killswitch.description: The product surface prioritizes recoverability: paid-but-no-access cases, provisioning failures, expired access, and support escalation have explicit handling.

items.title: Bounded Privacy Copy
items.desc: CyberVPN does not make an independent audited no-logs claim. Operational metadata may exist for security, billing, support, and provisioning.

items.title: Plan-Based Device Limits
items.desc: Device counts depend on the selected plan and must be enforced by the backend and support process.

items.title: Renewal Controls
items.desc: Automatic renewal appears only where the provider flow, user consent, cancel flow and evidence support it.

items.title: P2P Restricted by Default
items.desc: Torrent/P2P access is not advertised as broadly available. Node policy and provider rules control what is enabled.
